title: Vow Of Silence

jokes.text: Takes up two spaces, parks over the line. What kind of idiot-- Everybody's gotta move down now because of him.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about bad parking creating a chain reaction
jokes.punchline: The domino effect of selfish parking behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry opening - turning a minor social annoyance into righteous indignation. Sets up parking as episode theme.

jokes.text: You're throwing for yourselves, I might add. I mean, usually somebody else gives you a going-away party... But you've decided to give one for yourselves.
jokes.setup: Larry arriving at what appears to be a normal going-away party
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the social awkwardness of throwing your own farewell party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry observation - socially inappropriate but technically correct critique of party etiquette.

jokes.text: We're beloved.
jokes.setup: Larry's criticism of self-thrown party
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact justification for the breach of etiquette
jokes.explanation: Jeff's deadpan delivery makes this work - the confidence in his ridiculous justification is funny.

jokes.text: They don't take everybody? She auditioned, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry's apparent surprise that Juilliard has standards
jokes.punchline: Susie's defensive response revealing Larry's ignorance
jokes.explanation: Larry's cluelessness about prestigious institutions drives the humor here.

jokes.text: Why do you have to criticize my kid, Larry? I'm not criticizing. I don't think she wants to do it. I think you're pushing her into it.
jokes.setup: Susie accusing Larry of criticism
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately proving her point by criticizing more
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - denying he's doing something while actively doing it. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: What, are you making fun of me now? Yes. Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Susie asking if Larry is mocking her
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely honest admission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment of Larry's brutal honesty. The double 'Yes' makes it even funnier - he's enthusiastic about his mockery.

jokes.text: You know what the best thing about going to New York is? I'm not gonna see your face for three months.
jokes.setup: Susie building up to something positive about the trip
jokes.punchline: The 'positive' is getting away from Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie comeback - vicious but delivered with fake sweetness. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Why did you have to take up two parking spaces? You made me take two parking spaces, okay? Yes, I can understand that you're upset. I too was upset when I saw the red Volvo to my left also taking up two spaces. He created a domino effect.
jokes.setup: Larry being confronted about his parking
jokes.punchline: Blaming a mysterious red Volvo that supposedly started the chain reaction
jokes.explanation: Larry's elaborate excuse involving the phantom Volvo is absurd but delivered with complete conviction. Sets up running gag.

jokes.text: There's no Volvo out there.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate red Volvo excuse
jokes.punchline: Marty's simple factual correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that deflates Larry's elaborate excuse with simple truth.

jokes.text: There was when I parked, okay?
jokes.setup: Being caught in his excuse about the Volvo
jokes.punchline: Larry doubling down instead of admitting the lie
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - never backing down from an obviously false story. His defensiveness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, Vance. What's with you? What, have you got laryngitis?
jokes.setup: Vance not responding to greetings
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but wrong medical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Sets up the vow of silence reveal nicely - Larry's assumption is reasonable but wrong.

jokes.text: Vow of silence. Took a vow of silence.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to figure out why Vance won't talk
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous spiritual explanation
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of a vow of silence in modern LA social setting is inherently funny.

jokes.text: You took a vow of silence?
jokes.setup: Learning about Vance's vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction
jokes.explanation: Larry's disbelief is relatable - who does this in real life?

jokes.text: I've tried to get Susie to take a vow of silence. I have. I've talked to her about it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Vance's vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Jeff wishing his wife would shut up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - hen-pecked husband humor delivered with resignation.

jokes.text: That's never gonna happen. I have too many important things to say.
jokes.setup: Jeff wanting her to take a vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Susie's complete lack of self-awareness about her verbosity
jokes.explanation: Susie's complete obliviousness to why someone might want her quiet is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: See, that's funny. Meanwhile, he's trying to communicate it. It's just you're mouthing-- Oh, it's okay to mouth. It's okay to mouth.
jokes.setup: Vance trying to communicate while maintaining vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out the logical inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Larry immediately finds the loophole in the spiritual practice - classic rational Larry vs. illogical world.

jokes.text: You're still communicating, but in a much less effective way.
jokes.setup: Vance mouthing words during his vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical assessment of the futility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry observation - reducing spiritual practice to practical inefficiency. Economical and accurate.

jokes.text: He's an asshole.
jokes.setup: Vance supposedly can't speak due to his vow
jokes.punchline: He immediately breaks it to insult Larry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - the spiritual person immediately abandons his vow to talk shit. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: What's he doing here? He and his wife came. Oscar's really sick, so Susie felt like it'd be nice to invite him.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing the veterinarian at the party
jokes.punchline: The vet is there as a guest because their dog is sick
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of inviting your vet to a party because your pet is sick is peak Curb logic.

jokes.text: She's doing a chat and cut. A chat and cut? Really? She's feigning familiarity with someone she vaguely knows for the sole purpose of cutting in line.
jokes.setup: Woman appearing to greet someone in line
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific terminology and analysis of the social maneuver
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coinage - 'chat and cut' perfectly captures a specific social violation. Larry's expertise in social infractions shines.

jokes.text: Hi, first of all. Congratulations on a great attempt at a chat and cut. Really good. 99 times out of 100, that's gonna work. Unfortunately, I happen to be on the line so--
jokes.setup: Larry approaching the alleged line-cutter
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment approach to confrontation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry confrontation style - polite but devastating. The fake compliment before the takedown is brilliant.

jokes.text: Well, I mean-- I mean, to be fair, we met like eight years ago. I-- He remembers, so-- No, I-- I honestly don't.
jokes.setup: The alleged chat-and-cutter trying to justify her connection
jokes.punchline: The guy immediately admits he doesn't remember her
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - her weak connection gets immediately demolished by brutal honesty.

jokes.text: You think she's doing a chat and cut? I kinda feel like you are. Yeah. You see? He's been manipulated. You manipulated him, that's what it is.
jokes.setup: Larry getting backup on his chat and cut theory
jokes.punchline: The victim confirming he was manipulated
jokes.explanation: Larry's vindication is satisfying - getting the 'victim' to admit manipulation.

jokes.text: And look, you just did it again. What? No, I'm-- I'm-- Oh so, seriously, I'm not even gonna say anything this time. I respect your skills, really. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Woman trying to cut again after being called out
jokes.punchline: Larry's grudging admiration for her persistence
jokes.explanation: Larry's shift from outrage to professional respect for her line-cutting skills is funny character development.

jokes.text: Seriously, how much would it take for you to see, 'Eat Pray Love?' $3,000.
jokes.setup: Question about seeing a romantic movie
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculously specific and high price
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - putting an exact monetary value on his suffering. The specificity of $3,000 makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What if I brought a roach in? Listen, if you brought a roach in and you say you love that roach, I'm gonna do what I can to give it every chance it has.
jokes.setup: Larry testing the limits of veterinary care
jokes.punchline: The vet's professional commitment to treating even roaches
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of bringing a beloved roach to the vet, and the vet's sincere response, is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: You see a rat in the house, you don't want to kill it? Yes, if I see a rat in the house, I want to kill it. What if you injured it? Would you then bring it into his office for him to save it?
jokes.setup: Larry exploring the logic of pest vs. pet treatment
jokes.punchline: The ethical dilemma of injuring then trying to save the same animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - finding the absurd inconsistency in how we treat animals based on context.

jokes.text: I don't know why they don't have specialties. 'I'm a donkey vet. I'm a rabbit vet. I'm a turtle vet.' You know what I mean? In humans, there's an ENT, there's a podiatrist.
jokes.setup: Larry's confusion about veterinary generalization
jokes.punchline: His specific examples of proposed animal specialization
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical but absurd suggestion for veterinary specialization, with specific animal examples that sound ridiculous.

jokes.text: The way you're saying it, it's almost like there should be a doctor who handles just feathers. Yes. How does a vet do? What do they make, a guy like that?
jokes.setup: Discussion of medical specialization
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately pivoting to asking about salary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry transition from intellectual discussion to crude curiosity about money.

jokes.text: Okay, when is the last time you jacked off? Mmm. This morning.
jokes.setup: Larry inviting personal questions
jokes.punchline: The woman asking the most personal question possible, and Larry's casual answer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and payoff. Larry gets what he asked for, and his matter-of-fact response is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, three-legged race? Yeah yeah yeah. Hard-boiled egg in a spoon. Oh, what's-- Nothing more fun than that. Running with that thing?
jokes.setup: Tessler describing fun activities at the special needs event
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic enthusiasm for children's games
jokes.explanation: Larry's barely concealed disdain for wholesome activities comes through in his fake enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Sa-- Oh, you know what? I can't make it. How come? I-- I'm going out of town on Saturday. Oh, that's too bad. Where you going? I'm going to New York.
jokes.setup: Tessler asking Larry to participate in the event
jokes.punchline: Larry's quick lie to avoid the commitment
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry avoidance behavior - quick lie to escape social obligation. Sets up future complications.

jokes.text: I'm reading all this mercury stuff. I'm scared to get Sushi. I can't eat Sushi. How about some Italian? Wanna get some pasta? I don't like to have hot food for lunch.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Larry trying to decide on lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly specific and contradictory food restrictions
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic food restrictions escalating to the absurd - no mercury, no hot food, etc.

jokes.text: What about a peanut butter and jelly sandwich? I don't want bread. I don't want to have bread. Like a plate with-- Peanut butter and jelly just on a plate with a fork?
jokes.setup: Larry rejecting bread
jokes.punchline: Jeff's visualization of PB&J without bread
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd image - PB&J served deconstructed on a plate with a fork. Jeff's bewilderment is perfect.

jokes.text: Let me ask you a question. Did you notice, by any chance, if your wife was upset last night when you got home? No, not that I-- Not that I noticed. Did she mention my name?
jokes.setup: Serious moment about dog's terminal diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete self-absorption in asking about his own problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - completely inappropriate timing to ask about his own concerns during pet death news.

jokes.text: She doesn't care about you! You're not the center of the universe! Right now Oscar is! He's gonna die any minute now, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry's continued self-centered questions during pet crisis
jokes.punchline: Susie's explosive reality check
jokes.explanation: Susie perfectly articulating what the audience is thinking - Larry's narcissism at the worst possible moment.

jokes.text: Wouldn't it be nice for him to have a last meal? Oh, absolutely. I encourage it. So the dog could have a big chocolate bar? Dogs have last meals?
jokes.setup: Discussion of giving the dying dog a final treat
jokes.punchline: Larry's curiosity about the concept of dog last meals
jokes.explanation: Larry finding the parallel between human and animal death rituals interesting and absurd.

jokes.text: But neither of us have eaten since breakfast. I don't really give a shit about you. I care about Oscar right now, okay? He's in pain. He's gonna die soon. He needs his last meal.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentioning they're hungry too
jokes.punchline: Susie's brutal prioritization of dog over humans
jokes.explanation: Susie's complete dismissal of human needs in favor of the dying dog is brutally funny.

jokes.text: You drive a red Volvo? Don't drive a red Volvo. You sure? I drive a black Lexus.
jokes.setup: Larry still seeking the mysterious red Volvo
jokes.punchline: Another person who doesn't drive the phantom car
jokes.explanation: Continuing the red Volvo mystery - Larry's obsession with finding evidence for his lie.

jokes.text: You make as much as a regular doctor? Get me the fucking Pinkberry! Just curious. Okay. Just go! Hurry up! Time is of the essence! God.
jokes.setup: Larry asking inappropriate salary questions during crisis
jokes.punchline: Susie's explosive frustration at his timing
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible timing continues - asking about money while a dog is dying. Susie's fury is justified.

jokes.text: You're way outside the line. You're taking up two spaces. I'm like an inch over the line. No, you're a foot over the line.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting another bad parker
jokes.punchline: The disagreement over the severity of the parking violation
jokes.explanation: Larry's parking crusade continues - the precision of measurements in a petty argument is funny.

jokes.text: Society can't function like this. What? You have to be in the lines, the way it's supposed to be. Hey, here's an idea. Why don't you mind your own business?
jokes.setup: Larry's grand speech about social order
jokes.punchline: The man's simple dismissal
jokes.explanation: Larry's apocalyptic view of parking violations vs. normal person's reasonable response.

jokes.text: It's not an open field. It's not a farm, okay? Just-- Just park your car between the lines. Why don't you stay between the lines of your own goddamn business, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry's farm analogy for parking
jokes.punchline: The man's clever turning of 'staying between the lines' back on Larry
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - using Larry's 'lines' metaphor against him. Well-constructed comeback.

jokes.text: Why don't you stay confined within the lines of not being an asshole? 'Cause you're way over that line.
jokes.setup: The man's 'lines' comeback
jokes.punchline: Larry extending the metaphor further
jokes.explanation: Larry escalating the line metaphor into insult territory - good wordplay escalation.

jokes.text: The only person who's the asshole here is the pig parker. Pig parker? Yes. So you're calling me a pig?
jokes.setup: Larry coining 'pig parker' as an insult
jokes.punchline: The man's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good coinage - 'pig parker' is a memorable Larry-ism. The literal interpretation adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: I know what he's doing. He's about to do a chat and cut. It's a total chat and cut. No no no no no no. No no no. No, I know it looks like a chat--
jokes.setup: Larry being accused of the same thing he called out earlier
jokes.punchline: His desperate denial while doing exactly what he accused others of
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback - Larry being caught doing exactly what he called out. His denial is beautifully hypocritical.

jokes.text: I invented the chat and cut. This is amateur hour. If I was gonna do it, you wouldn't even notice.
jokes.setup: Larry being accused of chat and cut
jokes.punchline: His claim to have invented the technique and criticism of others' execution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - claiming expertise in the very thing he's being accused of. The professional pride in social manipulation is perfect.

jokes.text: I thought you were in New York. Just got back... This morning.
jokes.setup: Larry being caught in his New York lie
jokes.punchline: His quick improvisation about returning
jokes.explanation: Larry's quick thinking to cover his lie with another lie - typical escalation of deception.

jokes.text: Bedbug infestation on the nap mats. Oh my God. Bedbugs? Part of the event is this big, middle of the day nap-- All the kids. It's beautiful. A beautiful thing. A kind of pastoral feel to it.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Keegan's Club was postponed
jokes.punchline: The gross reality destroying the idyllic vision
jokes.explanation: The contrast between pastoral beauty and bedbug reality is genuinely funny gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Jesus, I can't believe it. I gotta go back-- I'm going back to New York. You're going back to New York? I'm going back, yeah, for like-- What-what-- what did you say it was? Six weeks on our end, approximately.
jokes.setup: Larry needing to escape the rescheduled event
jokes.punchline: Creating an even bigger lie about extended New York stay
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - when caught in a lie, make it bigger. The desperation is palpable.

jokes.text: Jerry and I are working on a new-- Oh. Oh, excellent. A new show. Yeah? Oh great. Great. Excellent. You know, don't-- Don't talk about that. With anybody.
jokes.setup: Larry inventing elaborate New York excuse
jokes.punchline: Creating fake secrecy around fictional project
jokes.explanation: Larry's lie becoming more elaborate - adding fictional Jerry collaboration and fake secrecy makes it more believable.

jokes.text: Mind if I take a bite of this? I do mind. That's for Oscar. Come on, one bite. No. Let me take one bite. What the-- Come on, I've got low blood sugar.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting to eat the dying dog's last meal
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate justifications
jokes.explanation: Larry's selfishness reaching new lows - wanting to eat a dying dog's final meal. The low blood sugar excuse is pathetic.

jokes.text: By the way... Mmm? Your taste was about twice as big as mine. What are you talking about? It was a small taste. No, it was about-- No, it was about as twice as big as mine.
jokes.setup: Both eating the dog's last meal
jokes.punchline: Larry's precise measurement of Jeff's portion
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry pettiness - measuring frozen yogurt portions while eating a dying dog's last meal.

jokes.text: No, my two bites were the same as your one. Oh, right. Okay. I can't even see what you're eating. Look at that! Look at the size of those bites you took!
jokes.setup: Argument over frozen yogurt portions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous measurement disputes
jokes.explanation: The argument devolving into precise bite measurement while eating a dying dog's treat is absurd.

jokes.text: You just took two bites! Bullshit. What do you mean, bullshit? I'm driving! Stop!
jokes.setup: The Pinkberry argument escalating in the car
jokes.punchline: Jeff having to focus on driving while Larry continues eating
jokes.explanation: The petty argument becoming a safety hazard while driving adds stakes to the silliness.

jokes.text: Oh, hey, Vance. I-- I don't know what the hell you're trying to say. Will you just talk, please?
jokes.setup: Encountering Vance again with his vow of silence
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued frustration with the mouthing
jokes.explanation: Continuing the vow of silence frustration - Larry's impatience with spiritual practices.

jokes.text: Pinkberry.
jokes.setup: Vance apparently trying to communicate something through miming
jokes.punchline: His single clear word about Pinkberry
jokes.explanation: Brief but effective - Vance's ability to clearly say 'Pinkberry' sets up future revelation.

jokes.text: They were closed. They're never closed. They're open till, like, midnight. We go after the movies all the time. They-- I'm telling ya-- Korean holiday. A Korean holiday?
jokes.setup: Explaining why Pinkberry was closed
jokes.punchline: The implausible Korean holiday excuse
jokes.explanation: Larry's improvised excuse about Korean holidays is absurd but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: I wonder if that'll catch on in prisons, you know, when they're about to be electrocuted. Pinkberry makes no sense. Well, why? The Pinkberry'll melt. It'll never get there in time.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pinkberry as last meals
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical concerns about frozen yogurt delivery logistics
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical analysis of Pinkberry as death row last meal - finding practical problems with absurd scenarios.

jokes.text: No, it's gross. It's full of my snot. Suz, just give it. Let me just throw it out. No no, just go-- Put the tissue in my hand. You know what? You've gone through enough today you don't need to be looking at garbage.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to dispose of his used tissue
jokes.punchline: His fake consideration masking selfish motives
jokes.explanation: Larry's fake concern about Susie seeing garbage when he just wants to hide evidence of eating the Pinkberry.

jokes.text: Where were you? I waited half an hour. You didn't confirm. There's no way I'm gonna go there. I didn't confirm? We had-- We had a plan. The plan is the confirmation.
jokes.setup: Larry angry about being stood up for lunch
jokes.punchline: Richard's bureaucratic approach to social plans
jokes.explanation: Good debate about social planning protocols - the distinction between plan and confirmation is relatable.

jokes.text: You're like some kind of government bureaucracy. You've got the plan. You've got the confirmation. You've got a subcommittee. I've got to go through all these levels. I'm not the Kremlin!
jokes.setup: Larry's frustration with Richard's confirmation requirements
jokes.punchline: Comparing lunch plans to Cold War bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turning a simple lunch confirmation into Soviet-level bureaucracy. Great imagery and buildup.

jokes.text: I'm just a human being who needs to be assured that if I'm gonna take a shower, get my clothes on and drive to a fucking restaurant, you're gonna show up.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued argument about confirmation
jokes.punchline: His detailed breakdown of the effort required for lunch
jokes.explanation: Larry's itemization of lunch preparation makes his need for confirmation seem reasonable.

jokes.text: A Shiva call for a dog? Have you ever heard of anything like that? I only met him once and he nipped at me. I shouldn't even be here. I didn't even like the dog.
jokes.setup: Being at what appears to be a funeral gathering for a dog
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical objections to mourning a dog he didn't like
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - finding the absurdity in dog funeral protocol and his honest assessment of not liking the deceased.

jokes.text: What the fuck is that? Is he like a mime or some shit? He's taken a vow of silence, yet he mouths. Like a ventriloquist without a puppet.
jokes.setup: Richard seeing Vance for the first time
jokes.punchline: The ventriloquist comparison
jokes.explanation: 'Ventriloquist without a puppet' is a perfect description of the absurdity of silent mouthing.

jokes.text: At the vet's I wanted to give him his last meal. He loved Pinkberry and Larry and Jeff went to get it and it was closed. And I feel such a sense of guilt.
jokes.setup: Susie explaining her guilt about Oscar's last meal
jokes.punchline: The audience knowing Larry and Jeff ate it
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Susie feels guilty while the audience knows the real reason Oscar didn't get his last meal.

jokes.text: There's a lot of bad karma attached to tattling. Are you aware of that? I wonder what your spiritual advisor would say about that.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to manipulate Vance
jokes.punchline: Using spiritual concepts to justify his lie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry manipulation - using Vance's own spiritual framework against him to avoid consequences.

jokes.text: Nobody likes a tattletale. Nobody. So go ahead and squeal and you'll rot in hell. Okay? So what's it gonna be?
jokes.setup: Larry's spiritual manipulation of Vance
jokes.punchline: Escalating to threats of eternal damnation
jokes.explanation: Larry's manipulation reaching peak intensity - threatening someone with hell over frozen yogurt is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Boy, for a guy that can't talk, he has a pretty big mouth, doesn't he? Yeah, well, he just mentioned you were still here.
jokes.setup: Larry learning that Vance told Tessler he was in town
jokes.punchline: Larry's ironic observation about the 'silent' person talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic observation - the guy who won't talk apparently talks plenty when it serves him.

jokes.text: Okay, so if you're going tomorrow, if I came back here next week and there's somebody that looks like you sitting in that chair, it's not you. I'm not gonna be in this chair, no.
jokes.setup: Tessler testing Larry's New York story
jokes.punchline: The specific imagery of Larry's doppelganger
jokes.explanation: Tessler's creative way of testing Larry's story - the visual of finding Larry's double is funny.

jokes.text: The red Volvo? Again. Jesus. What an asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing another badly parked red Volvo
jokes.punchline: His continued obsession with the phantom car
jokes.explanation: The payoff to the red Volvo running gag - Larry finally finds his scapegoat but it's a different situation.

jokes.text: Hey, Larry! I got your little note. Vance? Talking? Hey, Susie, guess what? Larry ate Oscar's Pinkberry! And Jeff was driving! Asshole!
jokes.setup: Vance's entire vow of silence throughout the episode
jokes.punchline: Breaking it spectacularly to expose Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode climax - Vance's vow was fake all along, and he saves the revelation for maximum damage. Brilliant callback integration.

jokes.text: Oh boy, am I so glad we're going to New York for three months and not gonna see your face! Mmm.
jokes.setup: Susie discovering Larry's betrayal
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic reference to his New York lie
jokes.explanation: Susie's final shot - referencing Larry's lie while expressing her hatred. Good episode-closing callback.

tmdb_overview: Larry's evolving alibi to get out of a charity function results in unexpected travel plans. Meanwhile, Susie makes demands for the terminally ill family dog's last meal; and Larry points out personal flaws in the buffet line and in parking lots.
